name: Corbinet Waterfalls Trail
name_original: Paraje El Corbinet — Circular desde Cortes de Pallás
description: Hike the moderate 4.4-mile Paraje El Corbinet circular trail from Cortes de Pallás, featuring waterfalls, clear pools, and diverse landscape
region_intro: Hiking around Cortes De Pallás offers access to a dramatically mountainous terrain, characterized by steep peaks, deep ravines, and large slopes. The powerful Júcar River carves spectacular canyons through the region, feeding the Cortes de Pallás reservoir. Hikers can explore areas with numerous waterfalls, such as those fed by the Arroyo de Cortes stream, and enjoy diverse landscapes. Best hiking trails in Cortes De Pallás The most popular hiking route is Corbinet Waterfalls Trail, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features clear pools and diverse landscapes around the El Corbinet area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cintos de la Cortada – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This trail includes historic sections and offers views towards the Muela de Cortes. Local hikers also love the El Corbinet Waterfall – Cintos de la Cortada loop from Embalse de La Muela, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and near the Muela de Cortes reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Cortes De Pallás is defined by dramatic mountains, Júcar River canyons, and numerous waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Cortes De Pallás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cortes De Pallás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there hiking trails that feature waterfalls in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The Arroyo de Cortes stream, particularly within the Barranco de la Barbulla ravine, boasts several, including Fuente Corbinet, Cascada de San Andreu, and Manantial de San Vicente. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e819944116/corbinet-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corbinet Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the picturesque El Corbinet Waterfall, a highlight often praised by hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Cortes de Pallás offers a dramatic landscape with steep peaks like Pico Martés and Muela de Cortes. You'll encounter deep ravines carved by the Júcar River, which also feeds the Cortes de Pallás reservoir. Notable landmarks include the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop a mountain with impressive views, and strategic viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the area.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Yes, Cortes de Pallás offers routes suitable for various fitness levels, including easier walks. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed in terms of amenities like playgrounds, trails leading to areas like Fuente Corbinet often have picnic facilities, making them pleasant for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Chirel Castle loop from Hoya de Chirel&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3 km hike that could be suitable for families.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Cortes de Pallás are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e819944116/corbinet-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corbinet Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cintos de la Cortada – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall – Cintos de la Cortada loop from Embalse de La Muela&lt;/a&gt;. These circular paths provide diverse scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Cortes de Pallás?
A: The hiking trails in Cortes de Pallás range from easy to difficult. There are 4 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes available. The terrain is dramatically mountainous, featuring steep peaks and deep ravines, so even moderate trails can involve significant elevation changes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt; are considered difficult, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any caves accessible via hiking trails in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Yes, the region is home to several natural caves. You can find Cueva el Tejedor and the impressive Cueva Hermosa, which is adorned with stalactites and stalagmites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that includes access to one of these cave systems.

Q: Do any hiking trails offer views of the Júcar River or its reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The powerful Júcar River carves spectacular canyons through the region, and many trails offer stunning views of it and the Cortes de Pallás reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Chirel Castle loop from Hoya de Chirel&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent vistas. Additionally, the Muela de Cortes Reservoir, part of a significant hydroelectric complex, is a striking feature visible from several routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall – Cintos de la Cortada loop from Embalse de La Muela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Cortes de Pallás?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic natural landscapes, the stunning Júcar River canyons, and the numerous waterfalls. The diverse terrain and the sense of being in a 'world apart' are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Cortes de Pallás can be enjoyed throughout much of the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during that season. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to hike year-round, though some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Cortes de Pallás?
A: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on the trails, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife like mountain goats and mouflon, or near livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Is parking available near the hiking trails in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around the village of Cortes de Pallás, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. For routes like those around El Corbinet, there are often designated recreational areas with parking facilities. It's always a good idea to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information if you're driving to a trailhead outside the main village.

Q: Are there any historical trails in the Cortes de Pallás area?
A: Yes, the region boasts historical trails that offer a glimpse into its past. The &lt;strong&gt;Senda de la Cortada&lt;/strong&gt;, also known as the Cavanilles Path, is a notable historic trail. It features old, sometimes cobbled, bridle paths that ascend with elegant zigzags towards the Muela de Cortes, traversing natural cornices and offering spectacular views. Portions of this path are included in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cintos de la Cortada – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Very striking cave a few meters from the Cortes stream. The story tells that a person who took care of and herded his goats lived there for a long time. A very picturesque and very quiet place.

The Arroyo de Cortes is a beautiful circular route of about 6 kilometers. Located in Cortes de Pallas, the route crosses beautiful waterfalls, pools of emerald water and numerous bathing areas with crystal clear water. Trail SL-CV 13

The Arroyo de Cortes is a beautiful circular route of about 6 kilometers. Located in Cortes de Pallas, the route crosses beautiful waterfalls, pools of emerald water and numerous bathing areas with crystal clear water.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Paraje El Corbinet trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Cortes de Pallás, so you can typically find parking within the village itself to begin your hike. There isn't a specific designated trailhead parking lot mentioned, but local parking options should be available.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered moderate and can be suitable for families with children aged 4-5 and up. However, some sections may require adult assistance, and it is not suitable for strollers due to varied terrain and potentially challenging parts like the 'Paso de la Franca'.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse range of terrains, including asphalted tracks through cultivated fields, rocky paths, and sections that run alongside or even within the Arroyo Cortes. The route also features typical Mediterranean forest and riverbank vegetation.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty. You'll discover stunning water features like the various waterfalls and crystalline pools of the Arroyo Cortes, including the impressive El Corbinet Waterfall and the Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall. You can also explore the Tejador Cave, an old refuge for livestock, and enjoy views of the Júcar River canyons and the Cortes de Pallás reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The warmer months are particularly inviting due to the numerous bathing spots and clear emerald water pools. However, be aware that waterfalls may be dry in autumn and winter. Spring and early summer offer lush scenery and pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for enjoying the water features.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the Paraje El Corbinet — Circular desde Cortes de Pallás trail.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no explicit mention of dog restrictions, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be prepared to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths, including the [CIMA VA06] Muela de Cortes, Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, and the European long distance path E4 - part Spain.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for challenging sections, sturdy hiking shoes are essential. It's also advisable to carry plenty of water, especially in warmer months, along with a hat and sunscreen. Some parts may require sure-footedness, and chains are present to aid in ascent/descent in areas like the 'Paso de la Franca'.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended direction to hike the loop?
faqs.answer: Many hikers suggest doing the route in reverse of the marked direction. This strategy allows you to save the refreshing water areas for the end of your hike, providing a welcome cool-down, or to enjoy the downhill views towards Cortes de Pallás.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming or wild bathing along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! One of the major draws of this trail is the opportunity to swim in the crystalline pools of the Arroyo Cortes. These natural bathing spots are particularly inviting during the warmer months, offering a refreshing break during your hike.
